item.title: context_args passed as word-split string — should be an array
item.intent: context_args is an array everywhere it flows: no word-splitting on unquoted variable, SC2086 warning is gone, filenames with spaces are safe.
item.description: context_args is built as a space-delimited string (e.g. '@file1 @file2 @file3') and then word-split at the call site via unquoted $context_args to pass multiple @file arguments to build_context. This pattern breaks on paths with spaces, triggers SC2086 from shellcheck, and is fragile. The fix is to use a Bash array (local -a context_args=()) throughout: build the array with += , pass it as "${context_args[@]}", and update run_agent() and run_groom_flow() signatures accordingly.
steps: Change context_args from a string to a local -a (array) in cmd_groom, cmd_review, and cmd_backlog_populate
Use context_args+=(...) to append elements instead of string concatenation
Update run_groom_flow() signature: accept context_args as a named array (via nameref or by shifting args)
Update run_agent() call site to pass "${context_args[@]}" instead of $context_args
Update run_agent() parameter 4 to accept variadic args or an array for context_files
Remove the shellcheck disable=SC2086 comment at line 1494 if it was added to suppress this
Run shellcheck — no SC2086 findings on context_args
Run: aishore groom, aishore review, aishore backlog populate — each must complete without error
Also convert groom_context string in run_sprint_loop (auto-groom path) to an array
acceptanceCriteria: context_args is declared as a Bash array (local -a) wherever it is used
No unquoted $context_args expansion exists in the codebase
SC2086 finding at line 1494 is gone from shellcheck output
aishore groom and aishore review still invoke agents with the correct context files
shellcheck passes with no new findings
